items.title: Paying rent for 'unsafe, unliveable' homes

children.content: Residents of the Indigenous community of Beagle Bay say they feel forgotten by their landlord: the Western Australian government.

items.title: H5 bird flu strain detected in Australian seabird, another WA case confirmed

children.content: Agriculture Minister Julie Collins says CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the deadly strain in a greater crested tern found in South Australia's Limestone Coast.

image.altText: Two hands holding two canisters with yellow lids and red liquid

items.title: Wildlife carers fronting WA's bird flu response left feeling let down

children.content: WA wildlife carers say the state government has forgotten them as PPE supplies and veterinary resources run dangerously low.

image.altText: A box of gloves, a packet of blue gowns and a box of masks being reached for by a hand.

items.title: Postal worker charged after suspected theft of AusPost mail

children.content: Australia Post worker Hussam Kassem is charged by police with mail tampering at a Perth Airport sorting facility, with $34,000 seized during a search of his home and car.

gallery.altText: A middle-aged man with greying hair, a beard and dark clothing, holding papers in his hand, walks out of a Perth court.
Side profile headshot of Hussam Kassem, wearing a black hoodie
Two AFP officers escort a man wearing a dark hoodie jumper down the driveway of a property with their backs turned.

items.title: Traditional owners say Noongar settlement ignores their rights

children.content: Traditional owners from WA's Wheatbelt say a historic native title settlement has increased division among Noongar groups.

image.altText: Three men stand talking to each other on a large rocky surface high above bushland under a cloudy, sunny sky.

items.title: 'Why do it?' Coaches lament taunts in Fremantle's furious comeback win

children.content: Luke Ryan and Dane Rampe are both penalised for taunting opponents in Fremantle's win over Sydney, leaving both their coaches unimpressed.

image.altText: Luke Ryan rubs the head of Chad Warner with his right hand

items.title: Six-year-old's love for garbage trucks earns him 'work' on bin rounds

children.content: Perth boy Thomas Aldworth has been welcomed as an official helper by the City of Perth waste management team and he even has his own uniform.

image.altText: Boy in hi-vis pulling wheelie bin

items.title: NDIS providers still operating in WA's north, despite complaints

children.content: Disability sector workers say lives are being put at risk due to the NDIS watchdog's failure to act on serious safety complaints in one of Australia's remotest regions.

image.altText: a man looking at a paper next to a computer

items.title: AC/DC fans celebrate what would have been Bon Scott's 80th birthday

children.content: AC/DC fans gather in Fremantle to celebrate what would have been the 80th birthday of Bon Scott, who passed away in 1980.

image.altText: A statue of a man holding a mic and singing.

items.title: Scorchers to begin Big Bash title defence in India in historic season-opener

children.content: The Perth Scorchers and Melbourne Renegades will open the BBL season outside of Australia — but the match is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit to India beyond 2026.

gallery.altText: Nick Hobson of the Scorchers and Aaron Hardie of the Scorchers pose with the BBL trophy

thumbnail.altText: Nick Hobson of the Scorchers and Aaron Hardie of the Scorchers pose with the BBL trophy

items.title: Australia's most played song on radio this week draws AI criticisms

children.content: A cover of the Madonna hit Like a Prayer is at the top of the charts but criticisms have been levied at the artist behind the cover, Josh Fawaz, over his suspected use of artificial intelligence.

image.altText: Madonna press pic, long blonde hair, silver jacket, blue pants, bare legs

items.title: The Rolling Stones prove 'This never gets old!' with new album

children.content: On album #25, Foreign Tongues, rock's elder statesmen once again dismantle concerns whether men in their eighties can still make great records.

image.altText: The Rolling Stones's Mick Jagger, Keith Richards and Ronnie Wood pose and smile together.

items.title: Mark McGowan's right-hand man parachuted into plum cabinet role

children.content: New Landsdale MP Daniel Pastorelli, the former chief of staff to two premiers, is set to become WA's newest minister after a meteoric rise through Labor's ranks and the resignation of Paul Papalia.

gallery.altText: Daniel Pastorelli looks into the distance while standing outside a building.

thumbnail.altText: Daniel Pastorelli looks into the distance while standing outside a building.

items.title: Workers' pay has not kept pace with productivity growth in 30 years

children.content: Research suggests that the typical worker's pay has not kept pace with productivity growth in Australia for 30 years.

items.title: PM to unveil AI agenda as government seeks to calm copyright concerns

children.content: A draft document, seen by ABC, reveals AI, gambling and the Middle East are expected to feature heavily in Labor's upcoming national conference debate.

image.altText: A man speaks at a lectern.

items.title: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win World Cup in any fashion

children.content: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

articles.title: Quick hits: It's the Mbappé show again, as England defender cops ban

children.content: An England defender pays the price for a terrible red-card tackle, and it's the Mbappé show in Boston as France advances. Here are today's quick hits.

children.content: Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé deliver France a comprehensive 2-0 win over Morocco to send Les Bleus into the World Cup semifinals.

items.title: Mining company hit with record fine for neglecting vulnerable bat in WA

children.content: Tyler's music continuously found newer, younger audiences thanks to it soundtracking memorable scenes in movies throughout the decades.
